text: The details of this visitation are obscure; the Hebrew text appears to be deficient, and it is difficult, in places, to attach any sense to the Septuagint Greek. An outbreak of haemorrhoids has usually been supposed, but this would explain neither the contagion nor the mortality; and it seems better to understand the passage as referring, at least in part, to bubonic plague. The ancients are believed to have associated mice (or possibly rats) with the outbreak of pestilence.
